You are working on the Slancha website at /home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/site.

Your job: fix SEO and meta tags in index.html, and create the public/ directory with essential files.

## What to do:

1. Create `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/site/public/robots.txt` with:
```
User-agent: *
Allow: /
Sitemap: https://slancha.ai/sitemap.xml
```

2. Create `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/site/public/sitemap.xml` with a basic sitemap for https://slancha.ai covering / and /contact.

3. Update `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/site/index.html` to add these missing meta tags (insert after the existing og:description meta tag):
- `<meta property="og:type" content="website" />`
- `<meta property="og:url" content="https://slancha.ai/" />`
- `<meta property="og:image" content="https://slancha.ai/og-image.png" />` (placeholder URL)
- `<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />`
- `<meta name="twitter:title" content="Slancha — Pick your latency, we handle the rest." />`
- `<meta name="twitter:description" content="Optimize inference with Slancha's latency‑first platform. Reduce cloud costs, boost performance, and simplify model deployment." />`
- `<link rel="canonical" href="https://slancha.ai/" />`
- Add `<link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />` in the head

4. Create `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/site/public/favicon.svg` — a clean minimal SVG favicon. Use a dark background (#121212) with the letter "S" in white or accent blue (#0A84FF), like:
```svg
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 32 32">
  <rect width="32" height="32" rx="6" fill="#121212"/>
  <text x="16" y="23" font-family="system-ui, sans-serif" font-size="20" font-weight="700" fill="#0A84FF" text-anchor="middle">S</text>
</svg>
```

5. Run `cd /home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/site && npm run build` to verify the build still passes.

6. Run `cd /home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/site && git add -A && git commit -m "[agent:claude] Add SEO meta tags (og:type/url/image, twitter card, canonical), favicon.svg, robots.txt, sitemap.xml"` to commit the changes.

Return a summary of what you did and the git commit hash.

2f5093dd-e98e-472c-9d85-61c70be70115.task:
Build a comprehensive OpenClaw observability prototype with:

**Phase 1: Data Collection System**
1. Create `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/observability/` folder structure:
   - `data/` - raw log files (JSON)
   - `scripts/` - data collection scripts
   - `reports/` - generated reports
   - `dashboard/` - frontend files

2. Build data collection script (`scripts/collect-metrics.sh`):
   - Parse session logs for spawn history, outcomes
   - Extract performance metrics (tokens, duration, model used)
   - Track skill installations via clawhub commands
   - Capture error patterns from logs
   - Extract learnings from memory files

3. Create cron job to run collection every 30 minutes

**Phase 2: Data Processing**
4. Build aggregation script (`scripts/aggregate-metrics.js`):
   - Merge daily logs into time-series data
   - Calculate performance metrics (avg duration, token rates, success rates)
   - Track skill adoption timelines
   - Identify error patterns (frequency, types, agents affected)
   - Extract lessons learned from memory files

5. Generate initial reports:
   - Agent spawn history (last 7/30/90 days)
   - Model performance comparison
   - Skill usage timeline
   - Error pattern analysis
   - Top learnings

**Phase 3: Dashboard Frontend**
6. Build HTML/JS dashboard (`dashboard/index.html`):
   - Tabbed interface with sections:
     * Overview (key metrics)
     * Spawn History (table with outcomes)
     * Performance Charts (duration, tokens, costs)
     * Skill Adoption (timeline visualization)
     * Error Patterns (heatmap + breakdown)
     * Learnings (timeline of insights)
   - Use Chart.js for visualizations
   - Filter by date range, agent, model
   - Export data as JSON/CSV

7. Add real-time updates (polling every 5 min)

**Phase 4: Custom Metrics**
8. Add interesting metrics:
   - Peak activity hours
   - Agent collaboration patterns (who talks to whom)
   - Model switching frequency
   - Learning velocity (how quickly learnings are applied)
   - Skill retirement tracking

**Deliverables:**
- Fully working data pipeline
- Live dashboard at http://127.0.0.1:18789/observability (or local static server)
- Sample reports from existing data
- Setup documentation

**Constraints:**
- Don't modify OpenClaw core files
- Use existing tools (cron, exec, read/write)
- Make it modular and extensible
- Keep it simple but extensible (no heavy frameworks)

**Success Criteria:**
- Dashboard loads and shows data
- At least 3 visualizations working
- Data collection runs automatically
- Clear documentation on how to add metrics

Excellent! Now let me provide a final summary to the main agent:

## Testing Suite Implementation Complete

I've successfully created a comprehensive testing suite for the Slancha website and user database. Here's what was delivered:

### **E2E Tests (13 test files)**
- **Authentication flows**: login, signup, password reset, auth callback
- **Contact form**: validation, submission, success/error states
- **Dashboard**: protected routes, API keys, usage stats, account settings
- **Marketing pages**: homepage, features, offerings, about, navigation
- **Accessibility**: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance with axe-core
- **Performance**: Core Web Vitals, load times, Lighthouse metrics
- **Visual regression**: screenshot comparisons across responsive breakpoints
- **Database integration**: auth, CRUD operations, RLS policies, security

### **Unit & Component Tests (7 test files)**
- Utility functions (validation)
- Custom hooks (auth context)
- React components (Nav, Hero, Footer)
- Page components (Contact, Login, Dashboard)

### **Configuration Files**
- `vitest.config.js` - Unit/component test configuration
- `playwright.config.js` - Enhanced E2E config with multi-project support
- `.github/workflows/test.yml` - CI/CD pipeline
- `vite.config.test.js` - Vite test configuration

### **Test Utilities**
- `scripts/seed-test-data.js` - Seed test database
- `scripts/cleanup-test-data.js` - Cleanup test data
- `scripts/test-database.js` - Database test runner
- `scripts/generate-baseline.js` - Visual baseline generator

### **Documentation**
- `TESTING.md` - Comprehensive testing guide
- `TEST-SUITE-README.md` - Quick start instructions
- `TESTING-SUMMARY.md` - This summary

### **Mock & Fixtures**
- Supabase mock
- React Router mock
- Test user fixtures

### **Key Features**
✅ Jest/Vitest for unit tests
✅ Playwright for E2E tests (multi-browser)
✅ Testing-library for component tests
✅ axe-core for accessibility
✅ Vitest coverage reports
✅ Full CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions
✅ Comprehensive documentation

### **Running the Tests**
```bash
cd site
npm install
npx playwright install --with-deps
npm run test                    # All tests
npm run test:unit               # Unit tests
npm run test:components         # Component tests
npm run test:e2e                # E2E tests
npm run test:a11y               # Accessibility
npm run test:visual             # Visual regression
npm run test:perf               # Performance
npm run test:db                 # Database tests
```

15d6bfdb-c9c3-4848-a1ec-6832e5c19d03.frozenResultText:
Perfect! I've completed comprehensive design research and positioning strategy for Slancha. Here's what I delivered:

## 📋 Research Deliverables

### 1. **Design Research Report** (`design-research/SLANCHA_DESIGN_RESEARCH.md`)
- **Competitor Analysis:** Deep dive into 6 major competitors (Databricks, Snowflake, CoreWeave, Lambda, Modal, Anyscale)
- **Design Trends 2026:** Dark mode dominance, gradient usage, real product UI over abstract graphics, workflow-based explanations
- **Brand Inspiration:** Color palettes, typography recommendations, visual language patterns
- **UX Best Practices:** The 4C framework (Clarity → Comprehension → Credibility → Conversion)
- **Actionable Recommendations:** Homepage wireframe, site architecture, specific improvements timeline

### 2. **Positioning Strategy** (`positioning/SLANCHA_POSITIONING_STRATEGY.md`)
- **Market Positioning:** Clear mapping of the polarized landscape (enterprise platforms vs. infrastructure vs. framework managers)
- **Value Propositions:** Tiered messaging for C-Suite, technical buyers, and product buyers
- **Competitive Differentiation:** Feature comparison matrix, positioning maps, "vs. competitors" messaging
- **Website Copy:** Complete homepage hero, workflow section, features, pricing, FAQ
- **Implementation Roadmap:** Immediate (1-2 weeks), short-term (1 month), medium-term (2-3 months)
- **Success Metrics:** Messaging effectiveness, brand perception, business impact KPIs

## 🎯 Key Insights

**The "Databricks of AI Engineering" frame wins because:**
1. ✅ **Familiarity** - Everyone understands Databricks' platform pattern
2. ✅ **Differentiation** - Clearly what we're NOT (not a data platform, not infrastructure)
3. ✅ **Aspirational** - We want to be the category leader
4. ✅ **Accurate** - We truly own the complete AI engineering loop

**Critical Differentiation:** Only Slancha owns the **complete automated loop** (evaluate → deploy → capture → post-train → repeat). Competitors offer pieces, not the integrated system.

**Design Recommendations:**
- Move away from generic enterprise SaaS aesthetics
- Use real product UI over abstract graphics
- Implement dark mode (non-negotiable for AI/ML tools)
- Show the workflow visually, not as feature lists
- Blue → purple gradient for AI innovation + enterprise trust

Both documents are now in your workspace and ready for implementation!

c7236d17-ad07-4de2-9f10-63c706480e02.frozenResultText:
## Summary

I've completed a comprehensive competitor analysis of the inference optimization and prompt routing landscape. Here are the key findings:

### **Market Landscape**

The market is fragmented across **3 distinct layers**:

1. **Evaluation & Optimization** (NotDiamond) - Prompt optimization, intelligent routing
2. **Gateway & Orchestration** (Portkey AI, OpenRouter) - Unified APIs, observability, routing
3. **Inference Infrastructure** (Fireworks.ai, Together.ai, Baseten) - Fast inference, GPU deployments, fine-tuning

### **Slancha's Competitive Advantage**

**White space identified:** No one owns **"pilot-to-production"**. 

Competitors either:
- Optimize prompts (NotDiamond) - but don't help you select the right model
- Provide gateways (Portkey, OpenRouter) - but for teams already in production  
- Offer inference infrastructure (Fireworks, Together, Baseten) - but don't evaluate which model to run

**Slancha owns:** Evaluation-against-production-workloads + structured pilot programs + continuous improvement loops

### **Key Strategic Recommendations**

1. **Position as "pilot-to-production platform"** - This is uncontested territory
2. **Concede these battles:** Model catalog breadth (Portkey), inference infrastructure (Fireworks/Together), custom model deployment (Baseten)
3. **Own these differentiators:** Production workload evaluation, 30-day pilot programs, continuous evaluation flywheel, outcome-based ROI

### **Deliverables Created**

✅ Full competitor comparison matrix (features, pricing, strengths/weaknesses)  
✅ User motivation analysis for each platform  
✅ Slancha positioning recommendations with messaging pillars  
✅ Competitive messaging strategies for each competitor  
✅ Go-to-market recommendations (target personas, sales motion)  
✅ Actionable next steps (0-30-90-180 day roadmap)

The complete 36KB analysis is saved at `/home/admin/.openclaw/workspace/competitor-analysis/competitor-analysis-2026-03-31.md`.

# 🚀 Slancha Design Concepts: Going Full FUNKY

I've cranked the creativity dial to 11. Here's a comprehensive breakdown of radical, boundary-pushing design concepts that will make AI teams do a double-take.

---

## 1. Five Radical Homepage Hero Concepts

### **Concept #1: "The Neural Glitch"**
**Visual Style:** Cyberpunk meets generative art. The hero feels like watching an AI's brain literally glitch and reassemble itself in real-time.

**Colors:**
- Background: `#0A0A12` (deep void black)
- Primary glow: `#7C3AED` (vivid electric purple)
- Secondary: `#EC4899` (hot magenta)
- Accent: `#14B8A6` (toxic teal)
- Text: `#F5F5F5` (off-white with purple tint)

**Layout:**
- Asymmetrical diagonal split (60/40) running top-left to bottom-right
- Left: Kinetic typography that morphs characters mid-word
- Right: WebGL particle cloud that reacts to cursor like a magnetic field
- Hero text floats at a -15° angle with subtle parallax depth
- A thin laser grid (5% opacity) runs across entire background, tilting with scroll

**Visual Details:**
- Text has `mix-blend-mode: exclusion` creating color-bleed effects
- Each letter has a 2-3px chromatic aberration (red/blue channel offset)
- Background particles pulse at 0.8s intervals with easing `ease-out-cubic`
- A "glitch bar" at bottom of text flashes every 8-12 seconds randomly

**Animation Timings:**
- Initial load: Text fragments assemble from 200ms staggered particle bursts
- Hover on CTA: Text expands 120%, glow intensity +200%, particles swirl toward button
- Continuous: Subtle RGB channel wobble at 0.05px frequency

---

### **Concept #2: "The Glass Data Stream"**
**Visual Style:** Holographic glassmorphism with flowing data. Think liquid interfaces floating in a cyberpunk void.

**Colors:**
- Background: `#0D0D14` (deep navy-black)
- Glass panels: Linear gradient `#1A1A2E` to `#0F0F1A` with 8% opacity backdrop-filter
- Accent gradients: 
  - Purple-to-cyan: `#A855F7` → `#06B6D4`
  - Pink-to-orange: `#F472B6` → `#FB923C`
- Text: `#E2E8F0` (soft white with cyan glow on hover)

**Layout:**
- Central glass "orb" (600px diameter) rotating slowly at 2°/sec
- Text wraps around the orb in a 3D curved path
- CTA buttons are floating glass shards (polygonal, not rounded)
- Background has 3 layers: (1) distant starfield, (2) flowing data ribbons, (3) floating geometric shards

**Visual Details:**
- Glass panels use `backdrop-filter: blur(20px)` with 1px white border at 15% opacity
- Data ribbons flow horizontally at varying speeds (15-30s loops)
- Orb has subtle refraction distortion on edges
- Floating shards have random rotation + position (parallax on mouse move)

**Animation Timings:**
- Data ribbons: Smooth sine wave motion, 20s duration, infinite
- Orb hover: Speed increases 3x, refractive index changes, particles accelerate inward
- Glass shards: Wobble at 0.3Hz with amplitude of ±2° rotation
- CTA hover: Shards rotate to align with button, glow pulses 3x before settling

---

### **Concept #3: "The Neon Mesh Network"**
**Visual Style:** A living, breathing neural network in 3D space. Nodes light up when clicked/hovered, connections pulse with data flow.

**Colors:**
- Background: `#050508` (almost-black)
- Mesh lines: `#4C1D95` (deep purple) at 15% opacity
- Active nodes: `#D946EF` (fuchsia), `#06B6D4` (cyan), `#F97316` (orange)
- Data flow particles: `#A78BFA` (lavender) streaking along lines
- Text: `#FFFFFF` with neon glow `text-shadow: 0 0 20px #D946EF`

**Layout:**
- WebGL 3D mesh occupies entire viewport (can be rotated 360°)
- Hero text floats ABOVE the mesh at z-index 10
- CTA is a "node" that when clicked, triggers a network burst animation
- Background has a subtle radial gradient (center is 10% brighter)

**Visual Details:**
- Mesh has 150-200 nodes, 300-400 connections (configurable density)
- Nodes pulse at different frequencies (1.5-4s intervals)
- Data particles travel along lines at varying speeds (0.5-2s per segment)
- Mouse movement creates a "magnetic pull" field (nodes bend toward cursor)

**Animation Timings:**
- Initial load: Mesh assembles from 0 to full density over 1.2s
- Node hover: Node expands 150%, turns bright white, connected lines glow 300%
- Click CTA: All nodes light up in wave pattern (100ms delay between waves)
- Continuous: Random nodes flash at 0.1Hz frequency

---

### **Concept #4: "The Holographic Portal"**
**Visual Style:** A portal to another dimension where AI models live. The interface feels like you're looking INTO the system, not at it.

**Colors:**
- Background: `#000000` (pure black)
- Portal ring: Radial gradient `#7C3AED` → `#EC4899` → `#14B8A6` (concentric circles)
- Portal core: `#0D1117` with animated grid lines (`#2D3748` at 30% opacity)
- Holographic text: `#E0E7FF` with cyan edge glow
- Particle swarm: `#A5B4FC` (light purple) and `#F0ABFC` (light pink)

**Layout:**
- Central portal (800px diameter) with 3D rotation
- Text appears to float OUT of the portal (perspective depth)
- Portal tilts and wobbles subtly (continuous 0.5° oscillation)
- Particle swarm orbits the portal like a ring system
- CTA is positioned below portal like a "control lever"

**Visual Details:**
- Portal ring has 12 segments, each rotates at different speed (0.2-1.5s per rotation)
- Grid lines inside pulse at 1.2s intervals
- Particles trail behind each other with 50-100px spacing
- Text has `text-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(196, 181, 255, 0.5)` + subtle float animation

**Animation Timings:**
- Portal entrance: Ring segments spin up from 0 to target speeds over 2s
- Hover on text: Text expands +5%, portal accelerates rotation 2x
- Particle orbit: Continuous circular motion at 0.3°/frame
- Click CTA: Portal compresses vertically 20%, particles converge, then explode outward

---

### **Concept #5: "The Kinetic Type Sculpture"**
**Visual Style:** Typography as sculpture. Words are 3D objects that rotate, flip, and reassemble based on scroll position.

**Colors:**
- Background: `#0A0A0A` (charcoal black)
- 3D text: Gradient faces (`#7C3AED` front face, `#4C1D95` sides)
- Ambient light: `#EC4899` (pink) from top-left
- Shadow: Radial gradient with `#000000` at center, `#2D1B4E` at edges
- Accent highlights: `#14B8A6` (teal) on edges

**Layout:**
- Hero headline is composed of 3D letter blocks (each letter is a cube)
- Letters are scattered initially, assemble into position on scroll
- Subtext floats in middle ground with parallax (2x slower than foreground)
- CTA is a "pressure plate" that depresses when hovered
- Floor has grid pattern receding into distance (vanishing point at center)

**Visual Details:**
- Each 3D letter has beveled edges with 2px highlight
- Ambient occlusion shadows under letters (soft, 30px blur)
- Light source creates consistent shadow direction (top-left)
- Floor grid uses perspective transform (1.5% skew)

**Animation Timings:**
- Scroll-triggered: Letters assemble over 1.5s with staggered delays per character
- Hover on letter: Letter flips 90° on Y-axis, flips back on mouse leave
- CTA hover: Depresses 5px with spring animation (0.3s duration)
- Continuous: Subtle floating at 0.2Hz (±2px vertical)

---

## 2. Motion Design Ideas

### **Micro-Interactions:**

**Button Hovers:**
```css
/* Neon pulse effect */
.btn:hover {
  animation: neon-pulse 0.6s ease-out;
  box-shadow: 
    0 0 10px #7C3AED,
    0 0 20px #7C3AED,
    0 0 40px #EC4899;
}

@keyframes neon-pulse {
  0% { transform: scale(1); }
  50% { transform: scale(1.05); }
  100% { transform: scale(1); }
}
```

**Scroll Progress Indicator:**
- Thin vertical line on right edge (3px width)
- Fills from `#14B8A6` → `#7C3AED` → `#EC4899` based on scroll %
- Particles trail upward from tip of progress line
- At 33%, 66%, 100%: Line pulses + expands to 5px (0.3s duration)

**Navigation Menu:**
- Links have skew transform on hover: `skewX(-10deg)`
- Underline animates from center outward (1s ease-out)
- On hover, adjacent links dim to 40% opacity
- Active section glows with `box-shadow: inset 0 0 20px #7C3AED`

**Form Inputs:**
- Label floats from input baseline to top-left corner (0.2s ease)
- Border appears only on focus (3px width, glowing)
- Focus border: `linear-gradient(90deg, #7C3AED, #EC4899)`
- On blur: Border shrinks back to 1px over 0.3s

**Loading States:**
- Skeleton screens are diagonal stripes moving left-to-right (1.5s loops)
- Stripe colors alternate: `#7C3AED` (20% opacity) → `#EC4899` (10% opacity)
- Pulse animation on static areas: `scale(1) → scale(1.02) → scale(1)` (1s duration)

### **Page Transitions:**

**Fade + Slide:**
```javascript
// Standard page transition
element.style.transition = 'all 0.6s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)';
element.style.opacity = '0';
element.style.transform = 'translateY(30px)';
// After 200ms delay, load new content
// Then fade in with reverse transform
```

**Glitch Reveal:**
- On page change, old content "glitches" out (RGB split + offset)
- New content assembles from horizontal slices (30px each, staggered 50ms)
- Total transition time: 0.8s

**Scroll-Based:**
- Sections don't appear all at once
- Content enters from left with `transform: translateX(-100px)` → `translateX(0)`
- Delay based on section depth (100ms per 500px from viewport)
- Staggered entry for child elements (50ms per child)

## 3. Color Palette Experiments

### **Palette A: "Neon Cyberpunk"**
```css
--primary: #7C3AED;        /* Vivid purple */
--primary-dark: #5B21B6;   /* Deep violet */
--primary-light: #A78BFA;  /* Lavender */
--secondary: #EC4899;      /* Hot pink */
--accent: #14B8A6;         /* Toxic teal */
--highlight: #F59E0B;      /* Electric amber */
--background: #0A0A12;     /* Void black */
--surface: #1A1A2E;        /* Navy surface */
--text: #F5F5F5;           /* Off-white */
--text-muted: #A1A1AA;     /* Muted gray */
```

**Use Case:** Primary color scheme. Purple dominates with pink/teal accents. High contrast, maximum impact.

---

### **Palette B: "Electric Ocean"**
```css
--primary: #06B6D4;        /* Cyan electric */
--primary-dark: #0891B2;   /* Deep cyan */
--primary-light: #67E8F9;  /* Sky cyan */
--secondary: #F472B6;      /* Soft pink */
--accent: #8B5CF6;         /* Violet */
--highlight: #FB923C;      /* Tangerine */
--background: #0D1117;     /* Deep navy */
--surface: #161B22;        /* Ocean surface */
--text: #E6EDF3;           /* Ice white */
--text-muted: #8B949E;     /* Steel gray */
```

**Use Case:** More calming but still energetic. Cyan/teal foundation with pink/violet accents. Great for data visualization contexts.

---

### **Palette C: "Magenta Madness"**
```css
--primary: #D946EF;        /* Fuchsia magenta */
--primary-dark: #A21CAF;   /* Deep magenta */
--primary-light: #F0ABFC;  /* Pink lavender */
--secondary: #06B6D4;      /* Cyan (contrast) */
--accent: #F97316;         /* Orange punch */
--highlight: #14B8A6;      /* Teal accent */
--background: #050508;     /* Pitch black */
--surface: #0F0F1A;        /* Deep purple surface */
--text: #FFFFFF;           /* Pure white */
--text-muted: #A78BFA;     /* Lavender muted */
```

**Use Case:** Maximum energy. Magenta as primary (unconventional!). Cyan provides strong contrast. Best for bold, attention-grabbing moments.

```css
--background: #0A0A0A;     /* Charcoal */
--surface: #18181B;        /* Dark gray */
--text: #FAFAFA;           /* Off-white */
--text-muted: #71717A;     /* Medium gray */
```

**Use Case:** Warm, energetic, different from typical "tech blue." Orange/magenta combo feels hot/passionate. Good for performance-focused messaging.
